MEVLÜT KARAKAYA (Ankara) ON BEHALF OF MHP GROUP - Mr. President, dear MPs; the one we are seeing.
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - This is exactly the case, do you know what you are talking about; You have been successful by holding your weapons tightly for forty years, it is true, you are right, you have been successful.
MEVLÜT KARAKAYA (Continued) - The bill is made by the Joint Committee on the Free Trade Agreement signed with the Government of Malaysia.
BERİTAN GÜNEŞ ALTIN ​​(Mardin) - It's so depressing, it's too much, because there is no war.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - We came from among the people, we came by walking, we came by talking about peace, we did not come by talking about weapons, you came by talking about blood, you came by making blood propaganda.
SABAHAT ERDOĞAN SARITAŞ (Siirt) - We always talked about peace, you always said "war".
ERHAN USTA (Samsun) - There cannot even be peace today. "We fired guns, we will fire guns again." You say, then you talk about peace.
CHAIRMAN - Dear MPs. Dear MPs.
MEVLÜT KARAKAYA (Continued) - .30 October.
CHAIRMAN - Just a moment, Mr. Karakaya.
Dear MPs, dear MPs, please look, I invited a friend of ours to the podium.
BURCUGÜL ÇUBUK (İzmir) - How we came here, how we were chosen without being out of line.
CHAIRMAN - Please, would you allow me, please.
I invited the MP to the podium, let him make his speech, please.
SABAHAT ERDOĞAN SARITAŞ (Siirt) - Well, they are talking nonsense, Mr. President, excessively!
CHAIRMAN - Here you go.
MEVLÜT KARAKAYA (Continued) - Mr. President, dear members of parliament; The bill we are discussing is regarding the approval of the Decision of the Free Trade Agreement Joint Committee dated 30 October 2024, signed with the Government of Malaysia. This proposal aims to eliminate some technical glitches and incompatibilities that occur in the implementation of the Free Trade Agreement, which has been in force since 2015 between two friendly and brotherly countries. Although this regulation seems like a technical update, it essentially constitutes an important link of Türkiye's Asian expansion vision and its understanding of balance politics in the world order we are in.
Dear MPs, this agreement is important as it is our country's first free trade agreement in Southeast Asia. Malaysia is one of the most important economic actors of its geography with its developing industry, advanced technology infrastructure and political stability. Thanks to this agreement, Turkish industrial products have easier access to the Malaysian market, and it is aimed that some products of Malaysian origin, especially electronic products, reach our country at competitive prices. This offer is not just a foreign trade agreement, it is an agreement that extends beyond Türkiye's own geography and strengthens its economic effectiveness.
It is a strategic step. As the Nationalist Movement Party, we believe that foreign policy should be carried out on the basis of national interests, within the framework of the principles of mutual respect, sovereign equality and multilateral balance. As our esteemed Chairman Devlet Bahçeli has repeatedly emphasized, Türkiye should not be a passive element of the unipolar world. Türkiye has to create its own axis with the strength it draws from its history.

Dear MPs, in order to understand the value and importance of these policies, I think it is useful to draw attention to a few issues related to the recent past. As you know, after the Second World War, a bipolar world order was established. This order, which lasted nearly half a century, ended with the collapse of the Soviets. In the new era called the Cold War era, the driving force of change and transformation was globalization. While the phenomenon of globalization, on the one hand, takes multilateralism as a reference, on the other hand, it has dragged the world into a unipolar hegemonic structure. The vision of a world in which everything would be fine, wars and conflicts would end, peace and tranquility would prevail, and inequalities would decrease in the new shrinking world as technology developed and spread to all areas of life was marketed. Nation state structures and even religions are the biggest obstacles to this change and transformation.
landed on target. The emergence of terrorist organizations to disintegrate nation-state structures and attempts to de-religiousize people under the guise of inter-religious dialogue coincide with this period. The discourse that nation states were outdated was one of the most important slogans of this period. Because, with the collapse of the Soviet Union, the walls built on the borders collapsed and there was no need for security policies. In this process, global governance institutions such as the United Nations, World Bank, IMF, World Trade Organization, World Health Organization, which were established after the Second World War and based on multilateralism, became unable to fulfill their functions properly and lost their missions. "The borders will disappear." Then today, more than 90 countries built new walls to protect their borders. Global inequalities have increased exponentially in many areas, especially economic and social issues. Proxy wars, the use of mercenary warriors, have replaced conventional wars. In the new order, multilateralism was replaced mainly by bilateral agreements. Tests and showdowns were the most prominent forms of behavior of this period. The basis of international law and legitimacy became power. So to speak, a powerful world order was formed. We see the most obvious example of this in Gaza. Information about the peace agreements regarding Gaza today, especially
The information that the first phase has been achieved is, of course, a situation that we welcome positively and that we wish and wish to see come to an end and ensure a permanent peace.
If we were to make an in-depth analysis; In essence, we see traces of the ideological and theological distinction conceptualized as Eastern and Western, which was made centuries ago with the reference to "othering" and updated with the same reference from time to time. In the bipolar political world order that began to take shape after the Second World War, the West was now strictly defined as Europe, the USA, Canada, and a part of Australia, New Zealand and South America, which are extensions of these countries, although they remain geographically in the east. In his work "From Culture to Wisdom", Cemil Meriç describes the economic landscape of this separation over the last few centuries as "East and West are two separate worlds, their issues are different, and as one gets richer, the other is doomed to get poorer." He summarized it as:

In geographical definitions, Türkiye has become the westernmost part of the East and the most eastern part of the West. In summary, in the post-Cold War period, the international system became unstable and economic, social and environmental crises increased. Israel's expansionist policies, especially in the Middle East, and conflicts in the region - including our country - pose significant risks to the region.
has been dragged. Türkiye's foreign policy is the manifestation of a multidimensional approach that is based on multilateralism and goes beyond a single-axis approach.
Dear MPs, we see Türkiye as neither completely East nor completely West, this is the historical fact of the matter. We define and describe Türkiye's foreign policy approach with an approach that looks to the East with one head and to the West with the other, with one foot in the East and the other in the West, just like the Seljuk double-headed eagle symbol. The basis of this understanding is Türkiye's ideal and claim to be the center.
Dear MPs, it is a fact that a new economic axis has emerged centered on Asia. We believe that this proposal we are discussing here today has a serious contribution to the formation of the Asia-centered axis.
Dear MPs, as you all know, the General Assembly is not open on October 13, but October 13 is the 102nd anniversary of Ankara, the headquarters of our War of Independence and the symbolic city of our national sovereignty, becoming the capital. On this occasion, I congratulate you again.
(The microphone was automatically turned off by the device)
MEVLÜT KARAKAYA (Continue) - Once again, I commemorate all our martyrs, especially Gazi Mustafa Kemal Atatürk, with mercy and gratitude.
I respectfully salute your noble delegation. (Applause from MHP benches)
